hero.question: Will Rafael López Aliaga win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
hero.prob_take: At 31%, the market is pricing López Aliaga as a legitimate top-3 contender in Peru's 2026 race, but that jump from 14% in 24 hours screams either new polling data or panic buying from insiders who know something. The real tell is whether this holds above 30% or collapses back down, which depends entirely on whether Peru's political establishment actually coalesces around him or if he remains a polarizing right-wing candidate in a fractious field where no single candidate breaks 35%.

daily_take.headline: Peru's Dark Horse Surges 17 Points. Smart Money Testing 31% as Ceiling or Floor?

The timing matters: Peru's 2026 race just got real with candidate consolidation and endorsement shifts. The market is probably overheating on momentum, but the upside catalyst (he locks the right-wing coalition) is real enough that shorting at 31% feels reckless. Savvy play: fade the euphoria on rallies above 33%, but don't get short. This man has real money and machine behind him.
